---
title: "Blog"
description: "Thoughts, notes and the occasional writeup."
listing:
  contents: posts
  type: default
  sort: "date desc"
  categories: true
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  feed: true
  fields: [date, title, description, categories, reading-time]
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
  freeze: false
---

## GitHub blog posts

Auto-pulled from repos whose README front-matter includes `type: blog`.

```{r github-blog}
#| output: asis
source("../_helpers/gh_type_cards.R")
render_gh_type_cards("blog")
```

## Posts from across my repos

Auto-discovered: any of my Pages-enabled repos that contains a file
matching `blog*.qmd` at its root (e.g. `blog.qmd`, `blog1.qmd`,
`blog2.qmd`) is scanned on every render, and each post appears below.

```{r cross-repo-blog}
#| output: asis
source("../_helpers/gh_type_cards.R")
render_blog_qmds_across_repos()
```

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## How to add a blog post to this section

Drop a file named `blog.qmd`, `blog1.qmd`, `blog2.qmd` … at the **root**
of any of my GitHub repos that has Pages enabled. Give it a YAML
front-matter block like:

```yaml
---
title: "A short writeup on mapping Brighton's AEDs"
description: "What we found when we joined OS AED data to population vulnerability."
date: 2026-05-20
image: thumb.png        # optional; relative path inside the repo or a full URL
# site: blog1.html      # optional override of the rendered URL — only needed
                        # if the project ships into a non-default location
# draft: true           # uncomment to hide while drafting
---

Body of the post goes here…
```

Render the project (most Quarto repos pick up new top-level `.qmd`
files automatically — just run `quarto render`), commit both the
`.qmd` and the rendered `.html`, and push. The next site rebuild
discovers the post via the GitHub Trees API, parses the front-matter,
and surfaces a card here linking to the live HTML at
`https://adamdennett.github.io/{repo}/{filename}.html`.

The daily 05:30 UTC cron means posts appear within 24 hours even if
nothing else is pushed to the website repo.
:::
